input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime.**
`StaticResource` resolves once at XAML load time. Switch to `DynamicResource` so bindings re-evaluate when the dictionary changes:

```xml
<!-- ✗ won't update -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryColor}" />

<!-- ✓ updates when you swap the dictionary -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryColor}" />
```

Then swap themes at runtime by replacing the merged dictionary:

```csharp
var mergedDicts = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDicts.Clear();
mergedDicts.Add(new DarkTheme());   // or LightTheme
```

**2. Activity restart on Android settings change.**
Android destroys and recreates the `Activity` when `uiMode` changes. Add `UiMode` to the `ConfigurationChanges` flag in your `MainActivity` so the OS doesn't restart it:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode       // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

Then detect the system change and react to it:

```csharp
protected override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);

    bool isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your ResourceDictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR** — `StaticResource` → `DynamicResource` for runtime updates; add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to prevent the Activity restart.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two .NET MAUI issues related to dark mode:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind colors from their ResourceDictionary themes. `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updates. They need to use `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When the Android system dark mode changes, the Activity is recreated by default. In .NET MAUI, they need to handle the `ConfigChanges` flag to prevent the activity from being destroyed and recreated.

Let me give a concise answer.
